reasoning: As of November 2025, there is no approved vaccine for Calicivirus (norovirus/sapovirus). The most advanced candidates (e.g., from Moderna and Sino Biopharmaceutical) are currently in Phase 3 trials. Moderna recently announced delays in its Phase 3 trial due to insufficient case accrual in the Southern Hemisphere, meaning they will continue the study into the Northern Hemisphere winter of 2025-2026 and only plan to file for approval in late 2026. Therefore, no vaccine will be approved by February 2026, the farthest resolution date. The only chance of a "Yes" resolution is a spurious, un-reverted Wikipedia edit that prematurely lists a vaccine in clinical trials, but Wikipedia's medical pages are usually strictly moderated. Following the instruction to increase uncertainty over time and keep probabilities within the 0.05-0.95 range, I assign the minimum 5% to the near dates and 7% to the February 2026 date.
